preconditions.description: Enrolled one full academic year before CPT begins (narrow regulatory exceptions apply)
CPT employment is integral to the established curriculum (required practicum, cooperative agreement, or course credit)
Cumulative full-time CPT tracked: 12 or more months of full-time CPT eliminates OPT eligibility at that degree level

timing_window.description: Per academic program; the DSO authorizes each CPT period on the I-20; no USCIS filing and no EAD is issued
timing_window.earliest: After one full academic year of enrollment (limited exceptions for certain graduate programs)
timing_window.latest: DSO authorization must be on the I-20 before CPT employment begins

preconditions.description: Degree level is completing (post-completion OPT tied to program end date)
Cumulative full-time CPT is under 12 months at this degree level
OPT not already used at this degree level; any pre-completion OPT used is deducted from the 12 months

timing_window.description: Application window spans the end of the academic program; work may begin only on the EAD start date with card in hand
timing_window.earliest: 90 days before program end date
timing_window.latest: 60 days after program end date; USCIS must receive the I-765 within 30 days of the DSO's SEVIS recommendation

preconditions.description: Degree level is completing; student never used CPT or used less than 12 months full-time CPT
OPT not already used at this degree level; pre-completion OPT used is deducted

timing_window.description: Same window as f1_cpt__f1_opt; direct path for students who did not use CPT
timing_window.earliest: 90 days before program end date
timing_window.latest: 60 days after program end date; USCIS must receive the I-765 within 30 days of the DSO's SEVIS recommendation

preconditions.description: Qualifying STEM CIP degree on the DHS designated degree list
Employer is enrolled in E-Verify and in good standing
Form I-983 training plan completed and signed by employer and student
STEM extension used no more than once per qualifying degree level (24 months)

timing_window.description: Timely filing auto-extends work authorization up to 180 days past OPT EAD expiry while the application is pending
timing_window.earliest: Up to 90 days before the current OPT EAD expiry
timing_window.latest: USCIS must receive within 60 days of the DSO's STEM recommendation and before the OPT EAD expires

preconditions.description: Selected in the H-1B cap registration (65,000 regular + 20,000 US master's; wage-weighted selection effective Feb 27, 2026 requires SOC code and OEWS wage level at registration)
Timely nonfrivolous petition filed within the 90-day window after selection
Petition requests change of status, not consular processing (affects cap-gap availability and $100,000 supplemental fee applicability)
Certified LCA for the worksite(s)

timing_window.description: Annual cap cycle: registration in March (FY2027: Mar 4-19, 2026, $215 per registration, selections by Mar 31, 2026); petition filing window ~Apr 1 to Jun 30; employment start Oct 1 or later
timing_window.earliest: Registration in March of the fiscal-year cycle; petition filing from selection notice
timing_window.latest: Petition within the 90-day filing window after selection; start date Oct 1 or later

preconditions.description: Selected in the H-1B cap registration (wage-weighted selection effective Feb 27, 2026)
Timely nonfrivolous petition filed within the 90-day window after selection
Petition requests change of status, not consular processing
Certified LCA for the worksite(s)

timing_window.description: Same annual cap cycle as f1_opt__h1b_active; STEM OPT validity often bridges to the Oct 1 start without needing cap-gap
timing_window.earliest: Registration in March of the fiscal-year cycle; petition filing from selection notice
timing_window.latest: Petition within the 90-day filing window after selection; start date Oct 1 or later

preconditions.description: Timely-filed cap-subject H-1B petition requesting change of status (not consular processing)
OPT active at filing extends status AND work authorization; filing during the 60-day grace extends status only
Employer is not cap-exempt (cap-gap unavailable for cap-exempt petitions)

timing_window.description: Activates automatically on timely filing; under the H-1B modernization rule effective Jan 17, 2025, cap-gap now runs to April 1 of the relevant fiscal year (previously Oct 1)
timing_window.earliest: Automatic on USCIS receipt of the timely cap-subject COS petition
timing_window.latest: Ends April 1 of the relevant fiscal year, or on petition approval start date, denial, or withdrawal, whichever comes first

preconditions.description: Timely-filed cap-subject H-1B petition requesting change of status (not consular processing)
STEM OPT active at filing extends status AND work authorization; filing during the 60-day grace extends status only
Employer is not cap-exempt

timing_window.description: Same automatic activation and April 1 boundary as f1_opt__h1b_cap_gap; STEM unemployment clock (150-day aggregate) continues to run
timing_window.earliest: Automatic on USCIS receipt of the timely cap-subject COS petition
timing_window.latest: Ends April 1 of the relevant fiscal year, or on petition approval start date, denial, or withdrawal, whichever comes first

preconditions.description: Cap-subject change-of-status petition approved by USCIS
H-1B start date reached (Oct 1 or the approval's effective start date)

timing_window.description: Status activates on the petition's start date; I-9 during cap-gap is documented with the H-1B receipt (I-797C) plus the cap-gap I-20
timing_window.earliest: Petition approval
timing_window.latest: H-1B start date (Oct 1 or later per approval)

preconditions.description: Beneficiary previously counted against the cap and in valid H-1B status
New employer files a nonfrivolous I-129 petition; AC21 section 105 portability permits work for the new employer upon USCIS receipt
Certified LCA for the new employer's worksite(s)

timing_window.description: Portability filing; new employment may begin on USCIS receipt of the transfer petition
timing_window.earliest: Any time while the beneficiary holds valid H-1B status
timing_window.latest: Before I-94/status expiry (or within the 60-day grace period following termination)

preconditions.description: Transfer petition approved by USCIS

timing_window.description: New validity period per the approval notice
timing_window.earliest: Approval date
timing_window.latest: Per approval notice validity dates

preconditions.description: Within the 6-year maximum stay (typically 3+3), OR an AC21 basis applies
AC21 section 106(a): 1-year extensions if PERM or I-140 was filed 365+ days before the end of the 6th year and not finally decided
AC21 section 104(c): 3-year extensions if I-140 approved but priority date not current

timing_window.description: The 240-day rule permits continued work for the same employer while a timely-filed extension is pending
timing_window.earliest: Up to 6 months before the current validity expiry
timing_window.latest: Before current status expiry (timely filing required to preserve the 240-day work authorization)

preconditions.description: Extension petition approved by USCIS

timing_window.description: New validity period per the approval notice
timing_window.earliest: Approval date
timing_window.latest: Per approval notice validity dates

preconditions.description: Material change: new worksite outside the original metropolitan area or a significant role change
New certified LCA for the new worksite; for placements, the amended-petition workflow is triggered automatically when a placement's worksite changes metro area

timing_window.description: Amendment must be filed before or at the time the material change takes effect
timing_window.earliest: As soon as the material change (worksite/role) is known
timing_window.latest: Before or at the time the change takes effect

preconditions.description: Amended petition approved by USCIS

timing_window.description: Amended terms effective per the approval notice
timing_window.earliest: Approval date
timing_window.latest: Per approval notice validity dates

preconditions.description: Employer green-card sponsorship decision made
Prevailing wage determination obtained and DOL-compliant recruitment completed before ETA-9089 filing
For AC21 106(a) benefit, PERM (or I-140) should be filed 365+ days before the end of the 6th H-1B year

timing_window.description: PERM may be started at any time; the filing date becomes the priority date once received
timing_window.earliest: Any time during employment
timing_window.latest: None, but file 365+ days before the 6th-year H-1B limit to preserve AC21 106(a) extensions

preconditions.description: DOL certifies the ETA-9089 labor certification

timing_window.description: Per DOL processing times; audits and supervised recruitment extend the timeline
timing_window.earliest: Per DOL processing
timing_window.latest: Per DOL processing

preconditions.description: I-140 filed within 180 days of PERM certification (PERM validity window)
Employer demonstrates ability to pay the proffered wage from the priority date

timing_window.description: Premium processing available (15 business days); priority date fixed as the PERM receipt date
timing_window.earliest: Upon PERM certification
timing_window.latest: Within 180 days of PERM certification

preconditions.description: Concurrent filing of I-140 + I-485 + I-765 (EAD) + I-131 (AP) permitted when the priority date is current
Priority date current per the governing Visa Bulletin chart (Final Action Dates or Dates for Filing, as USCIS designates monthly)
I-140 component filed within 180 days of PERM certification

timing_window.description: Concurrent-filing package models the section 7.4 concurrent filing edge case; retrogression after filing keeps the I-485 pending without resetting the priority date
timing_window.earliest: When the priority date is current per the governing chart
timing_window.latest: Within 180 days of PERM certification for the I-140 component

preconditions.description: USCIS approves the I-140 immigrant petition

timing_window.description: Per USCIS processing; premium processing 15 business days; the I-140 must not be withdrawn within 180 days of approval to preserve priority-date and AC21 benefits
timing_window.earliest: Per USCIS processing
timing_window.latest: Per USCIS processing

preconditions.description: Priority date current per the controlling Visa Bulletin chart (USCIS announces monthly whether Final Action Dates or Dates for Filing governs I-485 filing)
AC21 trap: once the priority date has been current for 1 year with no I-485 filed, further H-1B extensions are barred - file within the year

timing_window.description: File when the priority date becomes current; the platform alerts when the priority date is within ~6 months of current
timing_window.earliest: When the priority date is current per the controlling chart
timing_window.latest: Within 1 year of the priority date becoming current to preserve AC21 H-1B extension eligibility

preconditions.description: Consular processing elected instead of adjustment of status (affects cap-gap availability and $100,000 supplemental fee applicability for H-1B pathways)
Priority date current per the Final Action Dates chart for visa issuance

timing_window.description: NVC processing then consular interview scheduling once the priority date is current
timing_window.earliest: When the priority date is current per the Final Action Dates chart
timing_window.latest: Per NVC and consulate scheduling

preconditions.description: AC21 section 104(c): approved I-140 with priority date not current (per-country backlog) permits 3-year extensions beyond the 6-year limit
AC21 section 106(a): PERM or I-140 pending/filed 365+ days permits 1-year extensions
Barred if the priority date has been current for 1 year with no I-485 filed
I-140 must not have been withdrawn within 180 days of approval

timing_window.description: Post-6th-year AC21 extension per section 7.7 seed rules; 240-day rule permits continued work while the timely-filed extension is pending
timing_window.earliest: Up to 6 months before the current H-1B validity expiry
timing_window.latest: Before current status expiry

preconditions.description: USCIS approves the I-485; priority date current per the Final Action Dates chart at approval
During pendency: renew EAD and AP continuously; AP prevents abandonment on travel; retrogression keeps the I-485 pending without resetting the priority date

timing_window.description: Per USCIS processing; interview may be required; retrogression can pause final action indefinitely without invalidating the pending application
timing_window.earliest: Per USCIS processing once the priority date is current for final action
timing_window.latest: Per USCIS processing

preconditions.description: Immigrant visa issued at the consular interview and beneficiary admitted to the US as a lawful permanent resident

timing_window.description: Immigrant visa validity is limited; the beneficiary must be admitted before the visa expires
timing_window.earliest: Visa issuance after the consular interview
timing_window.latest: US admission before the immigrant visa expires

preconditions.description: Same-or-similar portability (AC21 section 106(c)): I-485 pending 180+ days AND I-140 approved
New job is in the same or a similar occupational classification as the sponsored position

timing_window.description: Job/employer change while keeping the pending I-485; priority date and application are preserved
timing_window.earliest: 180 days after the I-485 was filed, with an approved I-140
timing_window.latest: Any time while the I-485 remains pending

preconditions.description: Offboarding recorded with the employee's last day of employment; triggers the H-1B 60-day grace-period clock and notifies HR and counsel

Grace runs up to 60 consecutive days or until I-94 expiry, whichever is earlier; beneficiary remains in H-1B status but without employment authorization for a new employer until a portability petition is received

timing_window.description: Section 8 offboarding trigger: the grace-period clock starts on the recorded last day; escalating notifications go to employee, HR, and counsel
timing_window.earliest: Last day of employment recorded at offboarding
timing_window.latest: 60 days after termination or I-94 expiry, whichever is earlier

preconditions.description: Offboarding recorded: the OPT unemployment clock accrues against the 90-day aggregate limit counted from EAD start; days abroad count (narrow exceptions); cap-gap does not pause it
Separately, a 60-day grace period follows OPT EAD expiry

timing_window.description: Section 8 offboarding trigger for F-1 OPT: unemployment days accrue until new qualifying employment (directly related to degree, 20+ hrs/week) is reported
timing_window.earliest: Day after the last day of OPT employment
timing_window.latest: SEVIS termination risk once aggregate unemployed days exceed 90; 60-day grace ends 60 days after EAD expiry

preconditions.description: Offboarding recorded: STEM unemployment clock accrues against the 150-day aggregate limit, inclusive of the 90 days already used on OPT (does not reset)
Employer must report the employee's departure to SEVP within 5 days; material changes reported within 10 days

timing_window.description: Section 8 offboarding trigger for STEM OPT: departure report is due within 5 days; unemployment accrual continues until new qualifying E-Verify employment with a new I-983
timing_window.earliest: Day after the last day of STEM OPT employment
timing_window.latest: Employer departure report within 5 days; SEVIS termination risk once aggregate unemployed days exceed 150
